items.title: Train/validation/test splits
items.description: Proper splits define the boundary leakage must respect; random, stratified, and group splits are meaningless if preprocessors or features cross that boundary.

items.title: Cross-validation discipline
items.description: CV estimates generalization only when each fold refits preprocessors on training folds—typically via nested pipelines—not when global statistics are computed once on all data.

items.title: Feature selection timing
items.description: Selecting features using the full dataset or test labels leaks information; selection must run inside training folds or on train only before touching validation.

items.title: Generalization gap
items.description: Leakage artificially shrinks the train–test performance gap, mimicking good generalization until the model meets truly unseen production data.

items.title: Scaling and normalization
items.description: Standardizers learn location and scale from data; fitting them before splitting is one of the most common preprocessing leakage mistakes.

items.title: Outlier handling thresholds
items.description: IQR fences and z-score cutoffs must be computed on training data only; global bounds leak test distribution into training filters.
